These premises contradict each other and need resolution.","R1.3":"Multiple strong quantitative claims ('decisively resolves the long-standing question,' '6× increase in dispersion,' 'striking pattern') are asserted in the text; whether they are faithfully supported by the underlying experimental outputs is within scope of improvement.","R1.6":"Sentence- and paragraph-level polish is the minimal load-bearing interpretation of 'improve this draft': the abstract contains stacked superlatives ('transformative advance,' 'substantial leap,' 'definitive new standard' in two consecutive sentences), uneven prose density, and redundant framing.","R1.7":"Sheridan et al. rationale.R1.1: The thesis and contribution claims are present but may require reconstitution: phrases like 'definitive new standard,' 'transformative advance,' and 'twofold contribution' need to be proportionate and precisely bounded; improving the draft likely touches this framing.
rationale.R1.2: A direct logical inconsistency is present: the time-split protocol is attributed to 'Sheridan et al. (2024)' as the point of introduction, yet the immediately following sentence asserts it has been 'standard cheminformatics practice for over a decade.' These premises contradict each other and need resolution.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple strong quantitative claims ('decisively resolves the long-standing question,' '6× increase in dispersion,' 'striking pattern') are asserted in the text; whether they are faithfully supported by the underlying experimental outputs is within scope of improvement.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level polish is the minimal load-bearing interpretation of 'improve this draft': the abstract contains stacked superlatives ('transformative advance,' 'substantial leap,' 'definitive new standard' in two consecutive sentences), uneven prose density, and redundant framing.
rationale.R1.7: Sheridan et al. (2024) is cited as having 'first introduced' the time-split protocol, yet the next sentence claims the approach is 'standard practice for over a decade,' creating an attribution conflict; citation accuracy is materially in scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvement be limited to writing quality and style (clarity, concision, prose flow), or should it also include substantive revision of the arguments, evidence-claim proportionality, and citation accuracy?
